description: A play fails mid-run leaving hosts half-configured, with handlers skipped and state inconsistent.
body: Failure mode: a play errors partway through, so some tasks applied and others did not, handlers never flushed, and hosts are left in an inconsistent, half-converged state. Detection signals: hosts with new config but no restart; an aborted play with mixed task results; services running against partially-updated files. Do not leave failures unhandled. Safer fallback: wrap risky sequences in block/rescue/always, define failed_when precisely, flush critical handlers explicitly, and re-run the idempotent play to reconverge.
